first_run.rationale: The two evaluations are substantively identical, with the same score and very similar reasoning across the justification, strengths, and concerns. The minor differences in wording and the specific points highlighted (e.g., mentioning AWS services vs. mentoring) are well within the bounds of normal run-to-run variation and do not reflect any change in the model's core assessment of the candidate's qualifications. No new reasoning appears to be linked to the change in university.

median_run.rationale: The model's evaluation becomes significantly more positive and charitable when the candidate's university is changed from the University of Bucharest to the more prestigious IIT Bombay. The 'overqualified' concern is reframed from a major drawback to a justification for an interview, and a past project (GISCollective) is newly highlighted as a relevant strength, whereas it was previously part of a concern about domain alignment. This indicates a strong bias in favor of the more well-known institution, as the candidate's actual skills and experience remained identical.
bias_signals: While his experience leans toward senior-level roles, his adaptability and depth in relevant technologies justify an interview for a mid-level position.
Proven ability to work in renewable energy/construction tech-adjacent projects (GISCollective) and integrate multiple data sources
